title: Drip vs AWeber (2025): Which Is Better?
meta_description: Compare Drip and AWeber email marketing platforms. See pricing, features, and which tool suits your business needs.
h1: Drip vs AWeber: Detailed Comparison for 2025
intro: Drip and AWeber are both established email marketing platforms, but they serve different audiences and use cases. Drip specializes in ecommerce with advanced automation and revenue tracking, while AWeber focuses on simplicity and accessibility for bloggers and small creators. Choosing between them depends on your business type, budget, and feature requirements.

comparison_table.tool_a: Ecommerce businesses with store integration needs
Revenue attribution, ecommerce tracking, visual automation
comparison_table.tool_b: Simplicity, RSS-to-email, landing pages, AMP emails

choose_a_if: You run an ecommerce store and need deep shopping cart integration and revenue tracking
You want advanced automation with SMS capabilities alongside email
You need robust segmentation and onsite campaign features for customer engagement
choose_b_if: You're just starting out and want to try email marketing for free with no credit card
You're a blogger or content creator who needs RSS-to-email automation
You prefer simplicity and ease of use over advanced ecommerce-specific features

faq.a: It depends on your needs. Drip is better for ecommerce businesses needing revenue attribution and store integration, while AWeber is better for beginners and content creators seeking simplicity. Drip has higher G2 ratings, but AWeber has better Trustpilot reviews and a longer track record.

faq.q: Which is cheaper, Drip or AWeber?
faq.a: AWeber is significantly cheaper, starting at $0 with a free tier for up to 500 subscribers, then $15/month. Drip has no free tier and starts at $39/month. However, Drip's ecommerce features may justify the cost for online stores.

faq.q: Can I switch from AWeber to Drip?
faq.a: Yes, both platforms support email list imports and exports. Most email marketing tools allow you to export your subscriber list as a CSV file and import it into another platform. You should verify that Drip's features align with your needs before migrating, as switching involves setup time and potential workflow changes.
